metadata.source: Clinical pharmacology references: Stockley's Drug Interactions, FDA drug labels, WHO Essential Medicines

interactions.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic synergism: both inhibit coagulation pathways. Aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation via COX-1 and displaces warfarin from plasma protein binding sites, increasing free warfarin concentration.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid concurrent use unless explicitly indicated (e.g., mechanical heart valves). If unavoidable, use lowest effective aspirin dose (≤100mg/day), monitor INR closely, and watch for bleeding signs.

interactions.mechanism: Iodinated contrast media can cause acute kidney injury. In renal impairment, metformin accumulates and causes lactic acidosis.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Hold metformin 48 hours before and after contrast administration. Restart only after confirming normal renal function.

interactions.mechanism: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4, increasing simvastatin plasma levels by ~77%, raising myopathy risk.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Cap simvastatin at 20mg/day when co-administered with amlodipine. Consider switching to pravastatin or rosuvastatin (not CYP3A4 metabolized).

interactions.mechanism: Divalent cations (Mg²⁺, Al³⁺, Ca²⁺) in antacids chelate ciprofloxacin in GI tract, reducing oral bioavailability by 50-90%.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Administer ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids, iron, or calcium supplements.

interactions.mechanism: Combined serotonergic activity causes serotonin syndrome: hyperthermia, neuromuscular abnormalities, autonomic instability. Can be fatal.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated. Allow 14-day washout after stopping MAOI before starting SSRI. Allow 5 weeks washout after fluoxetine.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis, decreasing renal blood flow and methotrexate clearance. Methotrexate toxicity (bone marrow suppression, mucositis) can result.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs with high-dose methotrexate. If low-dose MTX for RA, monitor closely. Use paracetamol as alternative analgesic.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein and CYP3A4, raising digoxin plasma levels by 70-100%. Risk of digoxin toxicity (bradycardia, AV block, arrhythmia).
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Reduce digoxin dose by 30-50% when initiating amiodarone. Monitor digoxin levels and ECG closely.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit renal prostaglandin synthesis, reducing lithium clearance by 25%, leading to lithium toxicity (tremor, ataxia, confusion, renal failure).
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs with lithium. Use paracetamol instead. If NSAID unavoidable, monitor lithium levels every 5 days.

interactions.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, the enzyme that converts clopidogrel to its active thiol metabolite, reducing antiplatelet effect by ~45%.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Prefer pantoprazole or rabeprazole (weaker CYP2C19 inhibition) as gastroprotection. Avoid omeprazole and esomeprazole.

interactions.mechanism: Rifampicin potently induces CYP3A4 and glucuronosyltransferases, dramatically reducing plasma levels of ethinylestradiol and progestogens, causing contraceptive failure.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Use alternative/additional non-hormonal contraception during rifampicin therapy and for 4 weeks after stopping. Condoms are reliable backup.

interactions.mechanism: Both drugs reduce aldosterone-mediated potassium excretion, causing dangerous hyperkalaemia (K⁺ >6.5 mmol/L), which can cause fatal cardiac arrhythmias.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination unless monitored in specialist settings. Monitor serum K⁺ within 1 week of starting combination.

interactions.mechanism: Fluoroquinolones inhibit tenocyte proliferation; corticosteroids reduce tendon collagen synthesis. Combined risk of tendon rupture is significantly elevated.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination in patients >60 years, kidney/heart/lung transplant recipients, or with prior tendinopathy. Use alternative antibiotics.

interactions.mechanism: Both increase serotonergic neurotransmission. Combined use substantially raises risk of serotonin syndrome. Tramadol also inhibits serotonin reuptake independently.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination. If pain management required, use non-serotonergic opioids (morphine, oxycodone) with caution.

interactions.mechanism: Both drugs act on NO-cGMP pathway causing vasodilation. Combined use causes severe, potentially fatal hypotension.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Absolutely contraindicated. Do not use within 24 hours of sildenafil (48 hours for tadalafil).

interactions.mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase, causing accumulation of acetaldehyde after alcohol ingestion. Results in disulfiram-like reaction: flushing, nausea, tachycardia.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid alcohol during metronidazole therapy and for 48 hours after completion.

interactions.mechanism: Valproate inhibits epoxide hydrolase, increasing carbamazepine-10,11-epoxide (toxic metabolite) levels, causing toxicity despite normal carbamazepine levels.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Monitor for carbamazepine toxicity (diplopia, ataxia, nausea). Measure both carbamazepine and epoxide metabolite levels.

interactions.mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, the primary enzyme metabolizing phenytoin. Phenytoin levels can double, causing toxicity (nystagmus, ataxia, cognitive impairment).
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Monitor phenytoin levels closely within 3-5 days of starting fluconazole. Consider dose reduction of 25-33%.

interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, reducing theophylline clearance by 30-50%. Theophylline toxicity risk: nausea, arrhythmias, seizures.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Reduce theophylline dose by 30-50% when adding ciprofloxacin. Monitor plasma theophylline levels closely.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin potently inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, increasing tacrolimus bioavailability by 3-5x, risking nephrotoxicity and neurotoxicity.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination. If unavoidable, reduce tacrolimus dose by 50-75% and monitor trough levels daily.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, increasing atorvastatin AUC by ~80%, elevating risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Temporarily stop atorvastatin during short antibiotic courses. If statin must continue, use pravastatin or rosuvastatin.

interactions.mechanism: Additive pharmacodynamic depression of SA and AV node conduction. Risk of symptomatic bradycardia, heart block, and cardiogenic shock.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Contraindicated in combination IV/IV or oral/IV. If both oral needed, use with extreme caution and continuous cardiac monitoring.

interactions.mechanism: Trimethoprim inhibits renal tubular secretion of metformin (OCT2 transporter), increasing metformin plasma levels by ~50%, raising lactic acidosis risk.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Monitor renal function during co-administration. Reduce metformin dose in elderly or those with reduced GFR.

interactions.mechanism: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9 (primary warfarin S-enantiomer metabolism), causing 2-3x increase in INR within days.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose by 25-50% when starting fluconazole. Check INR after 2 days and every 3-4 days thereafter.

interactions.mechanism: Combined use associated with irreversible neurological damage in some case reports. Lithium may enhance haloperidol neurotoxicity.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Use lowest effective doses. Monitor for signs of neurotoxicity: confusion, extrapyramidal symptoms. Check lithium levels frequently.

interactions.mechanism: Both drugs prolong QTc interval via hERG potassium channel blockade. Combined use substantially increases risk of Torsades de Pointes and ventricular fibrillation.
interactions.clinical_recommendation: Avoid combination. If unavoidable, obtain baseline ECG, ensure QTc <450ms (male) / <470ms (female), monitor QTc daily.
